title: Tyler Dupy, CEO of Kansas Cattleman's Association
description:
Tyler Dupy is the CEO and Executive Director of Kansas Cattlemen’s Association (KCA). Based in Geary County, Kansas, KCA is a trusted advocate on behalf of farmers, ranchers, and rural America. Their working relationships throughout the industry make them a consistent and credible source for information with regard to top agriculture issues throughout the nation.

Dupy grew up in Northwest Kansas on a cow/calf and row crop farm and ranch. The family farm also included a custom harvesting company and hog farm. Dupy studied political science at Fort Hays State University and business management and logistics at Kansas State University. Prior to KCA, Dupy worked in executive project management in the steel industry.

Dupy is passionate about finding solutions to end the degeneration of rural America. He is staunch supporter of independent agriculture, arguing there is no replacement worthy of the sovereign farm and ranch. His core focuses are truth in labeling, checkoff reformation, competitive and transparent marketing, antitrust enforcement, infrastructure expansions, and banking and credit reforms.
